lines.text: I'm positioned on Monroe Street, west side, about two blocks south of Nakoma. It's {{CLOCK}} and we're {{ELAPSED}} in. Kill count Madison-wide is holding at 61 cumulative — that's 23 for this hour alone, up from 19 last year at this exact point.

East Side is still pulling weight, but I'm getting real-time pings that activity is shifting west. Hilldale corridor just lit up in the past eight minutes. Three separate incidents on University Avenue alone.
Weapons mix is interesting tonight. Bladed instruments up 34% in the west quadrant versus 2168. Economics are showing — ammunition tariffs are real and they're working.
I've got a residential barricade maybe 40 meters north of my position. Sandbags, reinforced frame, looks solid. No breach attempts yet, but the perimeter's drawn tight. They're watching.

Ticker update: Nakoma neighborhood is at 7 kills so far tonight, tracking 3% above 2168 pace. Lake Mendota shoreline has gone quiet after the earlier activity. Weather holding — visibility is degrading but wind's not pushing anyone indoors yet.
Hold — there's movement two blocks east. I'm repositioning to get a clearer sightline on University. The barricade crew is — they're pointing something and I need to...

tickerItems.text: Madison-wide cumulative: 61. Hour delta: +23 (vs. 19 YoY). Peak hours still ahead.
West side surge underway. Hilldale +34% weapons-shift incidents. Nakoma tracking +3% YoY. East Side holding steady.
University Avenue corridor seeing concentrated activity. Residential barricades holding on Monroe. Lake Mendota shoreline quiet post-22:00.
PurgeShield panels: Install verification hotline 608-555-0147. Don't assume your panels are oriented correctly. Check now.
